name: Week 3 Push (Chest/Shoulders/Triceps)
description: You own these lifts now. Bench at 55/side minimum, push toward 65. Swap: push press replaces strict shoulder press (uses legs to drive heavier overhead). Everything else stays — beat your W2 numbers.

exercises.notes: W2 working weight: 55/side (155 total). Start at 55, push for 65/side (175) on sets 3-4 if set 1-2 felt clean. You hit 65x2 in W2 squats — your bench can get there too.
W2 working weight: 50lbs. Aim for 4x10. If you hit it clean, try 55s next week.
NEW — replaces strict shoulder press. Small dip with legs, drive the weight overhead explosively. You can go heavier than strict press — try 45lbs, maybe 50. More athletic, more fun.
W2 working weight: 15lbs. If 15x15 is clean, bump to 17.5 or 20. Last set: drop set — do 15s to failure, immediately grab 10s for 10 more reps.
W2: Kinesis machine at 6. Try the actual pec deck if it's free. Squeeze hard at center, 2-second hold.
W2 working weight: 15lbs (too easy). Bump to 20. Single arm cross-body.
W2 working weight: 45lbs (cable column). Same setup — HIGH cable, kneeling facing away. Try 50 if 45 felt manageable.

tips: Push press is the big swap — uses leg drive to get heavier weight overhead. It's a progression from strict press, not a cheat.
Lateral raise drop set on set 3: go to failure at working weight, immediately grab lighter DBs for 10 more. Burns.
Bench: you're ready for 65/side. Even if you only get 4-5 reps, that's progress.
Cable lat raise: 15 was too easy. Bump to 20, no excuses.
Rest 3 min between bench sets, 2 min between push press, 75s between cable/isolation sets
Total volume: ~16 working sets for push muscles
